content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 17, 1992


Marc Goldstone
19 Brookdale
Irvine, CA 92714

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-452

Dear Mr. Goldstone:

This letter is in response to your request for confirmation
and confirms the telephone advice provided to you regarding the
campaign contribution provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").1

QUESTION

May you solicit contributions and offer a money-back
guarantee to every contributor?

CONCLUSION

Under the provisions of the Act, you may solicit
contributions and offer a money-back guarantee to every
contributor.

FACTS

You are seeking election to the office of Mayor of the City
of Irvine. You wish to solicit contributions and assure each
contributor that if the contributor is not satisfied with your
programs to improve the City of Irvine, you will return the full
amount of the contribution to the contributor.


1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-92-452
Page 2

ANALYSIS

All campaign contributions deposited in a candidate's bank
account are deemed to be held in trust for expenses associated
with the election of the candidate to the specific office for
which the candidate has stated that he or she intends to seek or
expenses associated with holding office. (Section 89510(b).) An
expenditure of campaign funds to seek office is within the lawful
execution of the trust imposed by Section 89510 if the expenditure
is reasonably related to a political purpose. (Section 89512.)

Campaign funds may not be used to make personal gifts unless
the gift is directly related to a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose. However, the refund of a campaign
contribution does not constitute the making of a gift. (Section
89513(f)(1).)2 Accordingly, the Commission has treated the refund
of contributions as any other expenditure of campaign funds; such
expenditures may be made to the extent permitted by Section 89512.
Accordingly, contributions may be refunded provided such refund is
reasonably related to a political, legislative, or governmental
purpose. (Hiltachk Advice Letter, No. I-90-053.)

We have previously advised that a pro-rata refund of
contributions has the requisite political purpose. We have also
advised that other circumstances would also permit a refund of
campaign contributions because of a reasonable relationship to a
political, legislative, or governmental purpose. (Hiltachk,
supra.)

You wish to make an offer to contributors that you will
refund their contributions if they are not pleased with your
programs. We perceive this promise to return contributions and
the subsequent return of contributions to any contributor who
requests the return of his or her contribution as reasonably
related to a political purpose. Thus, the return of contributions
is permissible in this limited setting, so long as every
contributor who solicits a refund of his or her contribution
receives such a refund and the refund of contributions is not
limited to select contributors.

_________________________

2 We caution, however, that this section does not permit or
authorize the refund of contributions to select contributors
absent a reasonable relationship to a political purpose.

Our File No. A-92-452
Page 3

     We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do
not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.³

                                          Sincerely,

                                          Scott Hallabrin
                                          Acting General Counsel

                                          By:  Blanca M. Breeze
                                               Counsel, Legal Division

SH:BMB:aa




3  Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.  Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.
